entry.title: SAP Inside Track Fortaleza 2024: Uma Celebração da Comunidade SAP no Nordeste
entry.content:
O SAP Inside Track Fortaleza em 2024 mostrou que a comunidade SAP no Brasil está mais vibrante do que nunca. Este ano, o evento bateu seu recorde de público, reunindo mais de 200 participantes em um espaço que ficou pequeno diante de tanta energia e entusiasmo. A comunidade SAP de Fortaleza e do Nordeste, que desde 2020 participa desses encontros voltados para aprendizado, networking e trocas de experiências, agora já pede um local maior para comportar o sucesso crescente do evento.

 Uma Semana de Conhecimento e Networking

Seguindo a tradição, o evento começou uma semana antes, com uma série de apresentações virtuais ao vivo. Esse Track online trouxe conteúdos em português e inglês, ampliando o acesso ao conhecimento para a comunidade. Quem quiser conferir esses conteúdos pode acessar todos os vídeos no canal oficial, onde estão disponíveis gratuitamente.

Durante o evento presencial, uma transmissão ao vivo pelo YouTube permitiu que pessoas de todo o mundo acompanhassem as palestras e interações do SAP Inside Track Fortaleza em tempo real. A transmissão foi dividida em duas partes (link da 1 parte e link da 2 parte) e está disponível no canal do evento, permitindo que mesmo aqueles que não puderam estar presentes no local tenham acesso a todo o conteúdo e experiências do dia.

Participar do SAP Inside Track ao vivo é sempre especial. No evento presencial, os participantes têm a chance de encontrar colegas com quem só interagem virtualmente e reencontrar amigos de projetos passados. Esse ambiente único promove um networking valioso e cria oportunidades para fortalecer laços profissionais e pessoais.

Além das conexões, o evento também oferece uma agenda rica de palestras ao vivo. O público teve a oportunidade de interagir diretamente com os palestrantes, fazendo perguntas e trocando ideias sobre os temas discutidos. Esse formato proporciona um aprendizado dinâmico e enriquece a experiência de todos.

A programação trouxe temas variados e palestrantes de peso. Confira abaixo alguns dos destaques:

  • MakeValue - Fátima e Gislâine - Projeto de Automação da Leitura de Extratos e Conciliação Bancária
  • Maycon Franco - Potencialidades da AI na migração S/4 HANA
  • Anselmo Milani e Bruno Lopes - Inovação Responsável:Integrando ESG na Estratégia de negócios com SAP
  • Igor Favacho - Stefanini - Cocriando soluções para um futuro melhor
  • Edson Thomaz - SAP Business Transformation Management
  • Marssel Vilaça - Compreendendo Valores Planejados na Contabilidade de Custos no SAP S/4HANA
  • Plauto Benevides e João Otto – Case de sucesso na Implementação S/4HANA M. Dias Branco
  • Lucas Ferreira - Tachyonix - Clean Core–O que significa? Como faz?
  • Sponsor CRiT - Roberta Vasconcelos e Renato
  • Jonys Arcanjo - IA Generativa e O futuro do Trabalho
  • Jefferson Alcântara - SAP Transportation Management - Overview
  • Luciano Casado - Using API with S/4HANA Public Cloud

Encerramento em Grande Estilo

Para encerrar o evento em alto astral, os participantes foram surpreendidos com uma apresentação de forró pé de serra, um dos ritmos mais autênticos da cultura nordestina. Foi um momento de celebração e descontração, onde todos puderam se divertir e comemorar o sucesso do evento.

Encerramento SAP Inside Track Fortaleza 2024

 O SAP Inside Track Fortaleza 2024 foi um verdadeiro sucesso, graças à dedicação e competência da equipe organizadora. Agora, a expectativa está ainda maior para as próximas edições, já que a comunidade SAP do Nordeste continua crescendo e mostrando cada vez mais interesse em fazer parte desse encontro tão especial.

A todos os organizadores, palestrantes, participantes e Patrocinadores, nosso muito obrigado! Este evento é uma prova da força da comunidade SAP no Brasil e da importância de espaços como o SIT Fortaleza para o crescimento e fortalecimento de nossos profissionais.

Links Importantes:

SIT Fortaleza

https://www.linkedin.com/groups/12467481/

Fortaleza - SAP Community

https://www.youtube.com/@sitfor-sapinsidetrackforta6071

Fotos: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1NEG0Lu3LnLO-5cDHwvunCfKuoq3abi6w?usp=sharing

entry.title: SAP CodeJam HANA ML in Wrocław 2024-10 Recap
entry.content:
At the end of October, we had the Getting Started with Machine Learning using SAP HANA and Python in Wrocław, Poland, kindly hosted by LeverX and organized by @Olena_Nadtochii. 

We had a fantastic group of participants, ranging from seasoned SAP veterans...

...to fresh talents who recently graduated from university.

This SAP CodeJam took place in a historical building—the former seat of the Faculty of Pharmacy of Wrocław University: a part of the university buildings' frontage, stretching along the Odra River in the northern part of the Old Town. The view from the top was breathtaking: from the Gothic Cathedral on the right to Concordia Design on the left—the Building of the Year 2021, according to ArchDaily.

And the food was equally fabulous.

I’d like to thank all the participants who joined me for this final SAP CodeJam of 2024!

The 2024 tour included 14 stops for me, and now it has come to an end.

Now, the next stops are in 2025, and I hope to see you in Spain ( @ajmaradiaga ), UAE ( @athavanraja ), India ( @Shilpa_Shankar ). Would you like to host such an SAP CodeJam in your location? Here are the details: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/quot-getting-started-with-machine-learning-usin...

entry.title: Unlocking Value in Mergers, Acquisitions, and Divestitures through SAP: A Key Enabler for CPG
entry.content:
Authors: Ayten Erbektas, Katie Nistad, Dmitry Melnik, SAP

Unlocking Value in Mergers, Acquisitions, and Divestitures through SAP: A Key Enabler for the Consumer Packaged Goods Industry

Heated debate has been ongoing for almost three hours. Ceylan, Head of Marketing and Sales, reflected on the Board’s deliberations to sell one of the company’s business units amidst recently declining returns. After all, she was the one who recommended to buy that brand almost a decade ago. But times change as do consumer preferences – and these products no longer experienced high demand across all retail locations of the company’s partners. Yesterday, Ceylan proposed to the Board another acquisition, of a high-promising consumer brand which would allow the company not only to reinvigorate its portfolio and consumer appeal, but also to diversify into an adjacent market. Directors gazed intently at Ceylan: it was her time to answer the key question of the day, “How ready are the company’s systems and processes to execute such a divestiture and digest such an acquisition?”

Our blog examines this topic and summarizes the experiences of multiple SAP customers who constantly rebalance their portfolio with the help of SAP solutions.

*             *             *

In today’s rapidly evolving Consumer Packaged Goods (CPG) industry, corporate combinations have emerged as a central growth strategy. Mergers, acquisitions, and divestitures (M&A/D) enable companies to expand their market reach, enter new segments, and drive revenue growth. However, managing these processes effectively is no easy feat. Companies face numerous challenges, from long due diligence processes and transition delays to under-realized synergies. This is where SAP S/4HANA, a powerful ERP system, can play a transformative role in helping organizations unlock the full potential of their M&A/D activities. Moreover, SAP solutions help monitor the optimal composition of the corporate portfolio: does brand A continue to perform? are we detecting early signs of the performance deterioration in brand B? why does brand C – while performing as usual – require even increasing investments which lower its ROI?  These and many similar questions are addressed by SAP solutions in real-time.

The Growing Role of M&A/D in the CPG Industry

In an industry characterized by fierce competition and shifting consumer preferences, M&A has become an essential tool for companies to remain competitive and grow their market share. Many CPG companies invest billions in acquiring new businesses to enter different markets, diversify their product offerings, or increase revenue. At the same time, divesting from non-core assets helps streamline operations and focus on strategic goals. Picture below displays M&A/D experience of just one of many SAP customers: it shows clearly that M&A/D is a big deal.

 

While the potential benefits of M&A are substantial, the process of integrating acquired businesses is complex and fraught with challenges. The integration of disparate systems, aligning corporate cultures, and realizing synergies in operations and costs are just a few of the hurdles companies must overcome. In many cases, companies struggle to meet these integration goals, leading to missed opportunities and suboptimal returns on investment. Transition Service Agreements (TSA) customarily executed between the buyer and the seller also present a significant cost item: the example above shows that just 10 acquisitions in 16 years cost the acquiror $470m in TSA and other PMI costs – in addition to over $8bn in purchase price.

To address these challenges, forward-thinking companies are turning to SAP S/4HANA, an ERP system designed to support the full M&A lifecycle—from due diligence to post-merger integration. SAP S/4HANA’s capabilities enable organizations to streamline processes, reduce redundant operations, and ensure data consistency, thus accelerating the integration process and unlocking value more efficiently. Many other SAP solutions provide solid foundation to execute M&A/D, including SAP Analytics Cloud, PaPM, SAP MDM and others. But to keep things simple, we will focus on SAP S/4HANA.

SAP S/4HANA: A Catalyst for Successful M&A Integration

Based on our experience in supporting customers’ strategic investments in SAP technology, we have seen how SAP S/4HANA can significantly enhance M&A outcomes. One of the key lessons from my previous role in post-merger integration is that the success of an M&A often hinges on how quickly and effectively a company can integrate its new acquisition into its existing operations.

 

The picture above is derived from the M&A experiences of many SAP customers and shows typical deal execution and consummation phases. So, how do customers utilize SAP solutions for their inorganic growth and portfolio rebalancing?  SAP S/4HANA provides a structured framework to manage M&A activities from pre-transaction to post-merger integration. Let’s break down how this system supports the three critical phases of integration:

  1. Addressing Immediate Priorities: One of the most urgent challenges after an acquisition is financial reporting. SAP S/4HANA Group Reporting simplifies this process by enabling companies to consolidate financial data from multiple systems, regardless of whether the acquired business uses SAP or another ERP. This ensures timely, accurate reporting and compliance, which is essential during the early stages of integration.
  2. Realizing Synergies: Once the immediate reporting requirements are met, the next step is to realize the synergies that motivated the acquisition. SAP S/4HANA helps organizations streamline operations by identifying and eliminating redundant processes, integrating procurement and supply chain functions, and extending shared services to the newly acquired entity. Automation is a key enabler in this phase, allowing companies to reduce manual tasks and improve efficiency.
  3. Driving Long-Term Growth: After the operational synergies are achieved, companies can focus on long-term growth by optimizing production, procurement, and other key business functions. SAP S/4HANA’s advanced analytics and production planning tools, such as MRP Live, allow organizations to make data-driven decisions that enhance efficiency, reduce costs, and improve product quality. This helps companies leverage their new acquisitions to drive growth and expand into new markets.

Flexibility in Cloud Deployment: Tailored to Your Needs

One of SAP S/4HANA’s standout features is its flexibility in deployment. Whether through public or private cloud models, it provides companies with the agility to tailor their ERP system to meet the specific needs of each acquisition. Smaller acquisitions can be onboarded quickly and efficiently through the S/4HANA public cloud, ensuring faster integration at a lower cost. For larger, more complex acquisitions, the private cloud offers more customization and control, allowing companies to address sophisticated processes while maintaining standardization where needed.

This two-tiered cloud architecture is ideal for companies that regularly engage in M&A activities, as it offers a scalable and adaptable solution to meet the varying demands of different acquisitions. SAP S/4HANA enables companies to onboard smaller acquisitions quickly while reserving the more robust private cloud for larger, more intricate integrations.

Success Stories: Driving M&A Value with SAP S/4HANA

Many leading companies have successfully used SAP S/4HANA to optimize their M&A processes and realize significant value from their acquisitions. For instance, global organizations in industries ranging from fashion to manufacturing have extended their shared services and automated key processes through SAP S/4HANA, driving operational efficiencies and enabling faster growth.

By integrating disparate systems, standardizing data, and automating core functions such as procurement and finance, these companies have been able to reduce costs, improve reporting accuracy, and enhance decision-making capabilities. The result? Greater agility, faster time-to-market, and improved financial performance post-acquisition.

 

The Future of M&A with SAP S/4HANA

As the CPG industry continues to evolve, consolidation will remain a key strategy for growth. Companies that can successfully navigate the complexities of M&A will be well-positioned to thrive in this dynamic environment. SAP S/4HANA offers the tools needed to unlock the full potential of M&A, enabling companies to integrate acquisitions more smoothly, realize synergies faster, and drive long-term value.

With its flexible cloud deployment options, advanced analytics, and automation capabilities, SAP S/4HANA provides the foundation for companies to stay agile, profitable, and ahead of the curve in their M&A endeavors.

Conclusion: Empowering M&A Success with SAP S/4HANA

Mergers, acquisitions, and divestitures are powerful tools for driving growth, but they also come with significant challenges. Companies need a robust and flexible ERP system to navigate these complexities and maximize the value of their M&A activities. SAP S/4HANA is designed to do just that—helping businesses streamline integration, unlock synergies, and position themselves for long-term growth.

In short, SAP S/4HANA is not just another ERP system—it’s a strategic enabler that empowers companies to realize the full value of their M&A strategies and stay competitive in the fast-changing CPG industry.

For those ready to explore how SAP S/4HANA can support their M&A journey, the opportunity is now. Unlock the value, stay agile, and drive growth with the right tools in place.

 

*             *             *

The Board voted unanimously.  Both the divestiture and the acquisition were approved and would happen very soon. Ceylan felt that this decision also marked the vote of confidence in her own team, which JAD relentlessly implemented modern IT solutions throughout the entire company. She sat in front of her team and business owners assigned to this acquisition.  Much work was ahead of them in the post-merger integration. But this would take the company to the next level in product and geographic scope. And there was so much else to accomplish throughout the PMI!,,,”

entry.title: Assista ao SAP Inside Track Curitiba 2024 ao Vivo e Online!
entry.content:
Quer se atualizar sobre as mais recentes inovações do SAP e melhorar suas habilidades em S/4HANA? O SAP Inside Track Curitiba 2024 oferece uma oportunidade única para aprender com especialistas em diversas áreas! Você pode acompanhar o evento ao vivo no YouTube ou assistir ao replay das palestras depois, caso não possa participar no dia.

Abaixo está a programação completa, com links diretos para as apresentações no YouTube. Compartilhe com amigos e colegas para que mais pessoas possam se beneficiar desse conteúdo de qualidade e se conectar com a comunidade SAP.

Programação Completa de Sábado, 23 de Novembro de 2024:

 

08:50 - 09:40 Check-in & Recepção com Café da Manhã 

09:45 - 09:55 Abertura do Evento

10:00 - 10:15 Patrocinador - Nova Consulting

10:20 - 11:00 NOVA Advisory: Insights que Impulsionam Resultados Reais - Lucas Kalache, Nova Consulting

11:05 - 11:45 Reforma Tributária - o que devo saber? - Thamires Kuwahara, SAP

11:50 - 12:30 Digital Supply Chain Planning - Integrando Estratégia e Operação - Paulo Fernandes, SCM-Design; Rafael Caiuta, REDDOP

14:30 - 15:10 Geração S/4HANA: A Nova Era do Consultor SAP - Leonardo Assumpção, ASSUMTEK Group

15:15 - 15:55 MOVE to SAP S/4HANA: Principais Desafios - Rodrigo Andrade, R2A e Cast Group; Matheus Paschoal, Discover

16:25 - 17:05 Migração para SAP S/4 e o Caminho para o Clean Core - Luiz de Godoy, Stratesys; Thiago Toma, Delaware

17:05 - 17:30 Encerramento e Sorteio de Brindes

Não perca essa oportunidade e acesse a playlist completa no YouTube para ver as palestras ao vivo ou assistir ao replay depois. E não se esqueça de compartilhar este evento com todos que desejam se aprofundar no universo SAP. Vamos juntos fortalecer a comunidade!

entry.title: Future Trends für GTM – 10 Jahre Global Trade Management
entry.content:
Wir feiern als DSAG-Arbeitsgruppe (AG) Jubiläum. In den letzten zehn Jahren konnten wir in der AG Global Trade Management (GTM) so manches bewegen – denken wir nur einmal an die Möglichkeit der Bestands-Umbuchung.

Doch bei unserem nächsten Treffen am 21. November 2024 in Hamburg schauen wir nicht nur zurück, sondern wagen einen Blick in die Zukunft von GTM. Welche Anforderungen haben wir z.B. an die SAP KI mit Joule? Welche GTM-Prozesse könnte die genAI aus unserer Sicht unterstützen? Und wir bekommen einen exklusiven Einblick in einen ersten Prototyp der Monitor Trading Contract Item App – auch hier dürfen wir unser Feedback kundtun. Last but not least beschäftigen wir uns mit der zukunftsweisenden Frage, welche Auswertungsmöglichkeiten in Form von Queries in einer Fiori App für GTM nötig wären.

Auch unsere „Sprechstunde“ mit SAP kommt nicht zu kurz. Platz für einen konstruktiven Austausch unserer aktuellen Verbesserungsvorschläge ist wie immer gegeben.

Sind Sie mit dabei? Zur Anmeldung für DSAG-Mitglieder geht es hier: https://www.dsagnet.de/event/gtm-treffen   

Herzliche Grüße,

Peter Langner

Sprecher AG GTM

entry.title: AI@FRE (Part 4) - Guardrail Enterprise AI Adoption – AI Architecture Guardrails
entry.content:
### AI@FRE Blog Series:

AI@FRE (Part 1) - Enabling Enterprise AI Adoption through the BTP FRE Engagement Model
AI@FRE (Part 2) - Accelerating Business AI Transformation with the FRE Value Framework
AI@FRE (Part 3) – Guardrails for Enterprise AI Adoption
AI@FRE (Part 4) - Guardrail Enterprise AI Adoption – AI Architecture Guardrails 

###

Scaling enterprise AI is no longer optional; it's essential for organizations looking to remain competitive. However, achieving this requires robust architecture and tool standardization to enable scalable, secure, and efficient AI deployment. Standardizing key areas in an organization, from security to automation, digital experiences, core, data, and AI architecture, sets a strong foundation for streamlined processes, enhanced agility, and a simplified landscape. 

The Importance of Standardizing Architecture and Tools for AI at Scale 

Building a scalable AI infrastructure hinges on standardizing architecture and tools, which enables enterprises to measure key outcomes like: 

  • Business Agility & Flexibility: Achieve XX-month reduction in time-to-market for new AI initiatives. 
  • Execution Speed: Drive XX-day reduction in project execution. 
  • Simplified Landscape: Foster streamlined architecture that minimizes redundancies and supports easy upgrades and scalability. 

Each area of standardization brings unique benefits and helps ensure consistent integration across SAP solutions, as we’ll explore below. 

 

  1. Standardize Security Architecture 

With AI adoption, ensuring security at every layer of the architecture is critical. A standardized security architecture enables cohesive, scalable, and adaptive access management. 

  • User Access Management (IPS): Enforces access control, minimizing security risks by managing permissions and restricting access to sensitive data. 
  • Single Sign-On (IAS): Unifies login experiences across SAP solutions, enabling employees to access tools seamlessly. 
  • Automated, Adaptive Access Management: Automates access protocols to ensure compliance, adaptive access control, and lifecycle management. 
  • Event-Driven Identity Lifecycle Management: Automates identity management processes in response to organizational events, streamlining updates to user access and permissions. 

Business Benefits: 

  • Unified Access: Simplifies security management by centralizing access across SAP environments. 
  • Secure Remote Work: Provides reliable access for remote employees without compromising security. 
  • Enhanced Employee Experience: Eliminates repetitive logins, allowing employees to focus on tasks. 
  • Productivity and Collaboration: Reduces friction in accessing necessary tools, fostering productivity. 
  • Digital Transformation: Lays the security groundwork for digital transformation efforts across the organization. 

 

Enabling of single-sign-on and identity provisioning throughout your application landscape 

 

  1. Standardize Enterprise Automation 

Automating workflows and processes is essential for scaling AI-driven enterprise operations. Standardized automation supports consistent, efficient, and error-free processes. De Agostini Publishing S.p.A leveraged SAP BTP to automate vendor invoice management, enhancing accuracy, efficiency, and data reliability across new markets. Another example is from Colsubsidio’s SAP BTP implementation, which enables them efficient, independent analytics for strategic, data-driven decision-making. 

  • Automate Visually: Utilize intuitive, drag-and-drop interfaces and AI to simplify automation. 
  • Integrate Seamlessly: Speed up development with pre-built workflows, bots, and connectors for both SAP and non-SAP systems. 
  • Collaborate Securely: Empower cross-functional teams with secure, agile development tools. 

Automation Examples: 

  • Generative AI for Interactive Modeling: Supports modeling through natural language inputs with proactive validation. 
  • Document Information Extraction: Automates data capture from structured documents like invoices. 
  • Action Process AI (SAP Signavio): Combines large process models with SAP expertise to improve accuracy and effectiveness. 

Key Benefits: 

  • Reduced manual effort and error rates. 
  • Increased speed and quality of automation. 
  • Empowered cross-functional teams with collaboration capabilities. 

 

Process AI at SAP Signavio 

 

  1. Standardize Digital Experience Architecture for Conversational Experience 

To create a seamless digital experience, the architecture must support modularity and scalability, allowing for a unified user interface across devices and applications. 

  • Conversational UX: Enable conversational interfaces across desktop and mobile, enhancing the user experience with intuitive, end-to-end interactions. 
  • SAP Start for Cloud Solutions: A modular homepage offers a single point of entry with pre-configured access to cloud solutions like SAP SuccessFactors and S/4HANA Cloud. 
  • Joule in SAP Applications: SAP’s digital assistant, Joule, is embedded in applications to enhance user experience, starting with SAP Start and SAP SuccessFactors. 

AI-powered Talent Intelligence: 

  • Talent Optimization: Use AI to generate job descriptions and interview questions tailored to organizational needs, streamlining HR processes. 
  • Skills-Based Matching: Empower skills-based organizations with talent intelligence to identify and promote internal talent efficiently. 

Standardize UX Architecture – The SAP UX Story 

 

  1. Standardize Core - Building a Lean Core 

A lean core structure is essential to keep AI-enabled systems agile and cloud-compliant, enhancing scalability and reducing potential disruptions. 

  • Avoid Custom Extensions: Minimize customizations to ensure compatibility with SAP’s upgrade cycles. 
  • Separate Extensions with APIs: Ensure extensions do not interfere with upgrades by leveraging APIs. 
  • Cloud-Compliant Extensions: Develop with cloud compatibility in mind, aligning with a three-tier architectural model. 

Business Impact: 

  • Smooth upgrades, minimal disruptions. 
  • Scalable, cloud-ready architecture. 
  • Enhanced reliability in deployments. 

 

  1. Standardize Data Architecture 

Data architecture standardization is vital for ensuring seamless data sharing, comprehensive analytics, and powerful AI model training across the enterprise. 

  • Business Data Fabric: An architecture that delivers contextually rich data to every user, preserving business context and logic. 
  • Intelligent Data Applications: Enable developers to create apps that are AI-driven, context-aware, and seamlessly connected to core business data. 
  • Extended Planning and Analysis: Unify planning functions for finance, HR, and operations within a single, data-rich ecosystem. 
  • Data Sharing & Products: Facilitate data-sharing with data products that provide both abstraction and usability layers without introducing new artifacts. 

Example Use Case: 

  • Business Decision Simulator: Simulate complex scenarios to predict potential disruptions and make data-informed adjustments. 

Core Benefits: 

  • Holistic data access across departments. 
  • Stronger alignment between operational and strategic planning. 
  • Enhanced data-driven decision-making. 

 

  1. Standardize AI Architecture 

The AI architecture must support diverse AI capabilities, from natural language processing to generative AI, ensuring seamless integration and adaptability across SAP applications. 

  • Joule in All Apps: SAP’s digital assistant Joule, embedded in SAP applications, offers users insights and support while automating routine tasks. It allows users to directly access services like SAP SuccessFactors and S/4HANA Cloud. 
  • Generative AI Hub in SAP AI Core: The GenAI Hub provides a central location for generative AI models, enabling SAP applications to draw upon AI capabilities like text generation, summarization, and process modeling in real time. 
  • SAP-Hosted Foundation Models: By hosting foundational AI models, SAP AI Core offers scalability and reliability, reducing dependency on external AI solutions and ensuring secure data handling. 

Benefits: 

  • Scalability: Supports consistent deployment across all SAP applications, reducing the need for fragmented, custom AI solutions. 
  • Data Security: Centralizes model hosting within SAP’s secure environment. 
  • Enhanced Process Intelligence: Infuses advanced process and data understanding across SAP systems, allowing companies to optimize AI-driven workflows effectively. 

Conclusion 

Standardizing architecture and tools across these six key areas creates a solid foundation for scalable, secure, and efficient AI adoption in enterprise settings. This standardization empowers organizations to move faster, adapt more readily to changes, and achieve a simplified and agile landscape that supports continued innovation and growth. 

 

Curious on a structured customer engagement model to set up standardized Cloud Architecture for Business AI Adoption? Connect with our Global BTP Customer Engagement Team at BTPFRE@sap.com.

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Porto 2025: Exploring the Future of SAP Innovation
entry.content:
SAP Inside Track Porto 2025: Exploring the Future of SAP Innovation

The SAP Inside Track Porto 2025 promises to be a landmark event in the SAP community, bringing together experts, thought leaders, and SAP enthusiasts from all over the globe to explore the cutting-edge innovations that are reshaping the future of enterprise technology. Hosted at the The Lodge Hotel Porto on September 27th, 2025, the event will dive into some of the most transformative trends in SAP technology today, including the migration to S/4HANA, advancements in Generative AI (Gen AI), the rise of Instant Payments, and the pursuit of Green Ledger solutions for sustainable business practices.

In this article, we’ll give you a sneak peek into the key topics set to be discussed, why they matter for businesses of all sizes, and how SAP is playing a central role in the evolution of enterprise technology.

  • The SAP Revolution: From S/4HANA to the Future

One of the most pressing topics for businesses worldwide is the migration to SAP S/4HANA. As SAP continues its push toward a cloud-first future, migrating from legacy systems to S/4HANA is no longer just a technical upgrade; it’s a strategic move that can unlock new capabilities, streamline operations, and improve business outcomes.

At SAP Inside Track Porto 2025, experts will share insights into the S/4HANA migration journey, focusing on the different approaches organizations can take. The discussion will address the Greenfield vs. Brownfield strategies, helping businesses choose the best path based on their current systems and goals. Presenters will also explore the importance of business process transformation during the migration, as well as the critical role that the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) plays in connecting systems, driving innovation, and ensuring a smooth transition to the cloud.

As part of the Intelligent Enterprise framework, SAP S/4HANA empowers businesses to automate processes, gain real-time insights, and improve collaboration. Whether you're just starting your migration journey or looking to optimize an existing S/4HANA system, SAP Inside Track Porto 2025 will provide the tools, knowledge, and strategies needed to make the most of this powerful platform.

  • Generative AI: Unlocking New Possibilities

The rise of Generative AI (Gen AI) is one of the most exciting advancements in the tech world today, and it’s quickly making its way into SAP solutions. At SAP Inside Track Porto 2025, Gen AI will take center stage as a key area of discussion. With applications ranging from enhanced decision-making to automated workflows, Generative AI is transforming how businesses use data to drive innovation and improve performance.

SAP is already incorporating AI-driven solutions into its S/4HANA and SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) offerings. SAP Business AI and SAP Copilot are just a few examples of how AI is being embedded within SAP applications to enhance user experiences, optimize operations, and deliver personalized insights. Whether it’s automating manual tasks, predicting market trends, or creating advanced analytics models, Gen AI can empower organizations to make smarter, faster decisions.

At the event, attendees will gain hands-on experience with SAP AI Core and AI Foundation, SAP’s tools designed to help businesses deploy and scale AI models across various applications. The conversations around AI in the enterprise will focus on how SAP is helping organizations leverage AI to drive growth, efficiency, and competitive advantage in an increasingly automated world.

  • Instant Payments: A New Era of Financial Transactions

As businesses and consumers alike demand faster and more secure payment methods, Instant Payments are becoming a critical theme for SAP and the broader financial ecosystem. SAP's instant payment solutions, built on advanced cloud technologies, are revolutionizing how companies handle transactions, particularly in industries like retail, banking, and manufacturing.

During SAP Inside Track Porto 2025, sessions will focus on how SAP is helping businesses adopt and integrate real-time payment capabilities into their financial systems. Experts will dive into the benefits of instant payments, including:

  • Faster cash flow management for businesses
  • Increased transaction transparency and security
  • Improved customer experience through seamless payment processing

With the world becoming more interconnected and digital-first, businesses can no longer afford to wait days for payments to clear. The discussions at SAP Inside Track Porto 2025 will provide attendees with the latest best practices for implementing instant payments into their enterprise systems, helping organizations stay competitive in a fast-paced, real-time economy.

  • Green Ledger: Sustainability as a Business Imperative

As environmental concerns grow, sustainability has become a top priority for organizations worldwide. Businesses are under increasing pressure to reduce their carbon footprints, optimize resource usage, and contribute positively to society. SAP is leading the charge in integrating sustainability into its software solutions, and Green Ledger is one of the most powerful tools in this effort.

SAP’s Green Ledger enables businesses to track, measure, and report their environmental impact, providing a comprehensive view of energy consumption, carbon emissions, and other key sustainability metrics.

Through the use of SAP solutions like SAP Cloud for Sustainable Enterprises, companies can access the data and insights they need to make more sustainable decisions across the entire supply chain. At the event, attendees will learn how to leverage SAP’s sustainability capabilities to:

  • Monitor and reduce carbon emissions
  • Track supply chain sustainability
  • Report ESG metrics to stakeholders in line with international standards

As global regulations around sustainability and environmental impact tighten, organizations need reliable tools to ensure they’re meeting compliance requirements. SAP Inside Track Porto 2025 will equip businesses with the knowledge and solutions they need to integrate sustainability into their core operations.

  • Networking and Collaboration: The Power of the SAP Community

SAP Inside Track events are not just about learning from experts – they’re about connecting with the global SAP community. SAP Inside Track Porto 2025 will bring together SAP professionals from all industries and regions, creating opportunities to share ideas, discuss challenges, and build lasting relationships. Whether you're an SAP developer, consultant, or business user, the event will offer a platform to engage with like-minded individuals and expand your professional network.

Collaborating with peers will be a central theme of the event. Attendees will have the chance to join hands-on workshops, participate in live demos, and ask questions during panel discussions. The event will also feature use case presentations, where real-world organizations share how they’ve successfully implemented SAP innovations to drive business transformation.

Conclusion: The Future is Now

SAP Inside Track Porto 2025 is not just an event; it’s a unique opportunity to dive deep into the most exciting innovations that are shaping the future of enterprise technology. From S/4HANA migration and Generative AI to Instant Payments and Green Ledger solutions, the event will provide invaluable insights into how SAP is helping businesses transform, optimize, and thrive in the digital era.

Whether you’re looking to accelerate your digital transformation, explore new technologies, or network with SAP experts and peers, SAP Inside Track Porto 2025 is the place to be. Don’t miss out on this incredible opportunity to be at the forefront of SAP innovation!

Mark your calendars – September 27th, 2025 – The Lodge Hotel Porto. We’ll see you there!

entry.title: Sydney Stammtisch Recap 2411
entry.content:
Thanks to everyone who's turned out for a Sydney SAP Stammtisch in 2024. We wrapped things up with a great event at The Paragon Hotel last Thursday night.  We had about 35 attendees, which is terrific. That seems to be the new normal turnout for us

Ranjeet

entry.title: Support Processes and Escalation Paths
entry.content:
What are the support processes and how do you go about resolving an issue that has arisen? 

Find a solution to an issue by searching SAP’s many platforms which are listed below: 

SAP for me  

SAP Help Portal 

SAP Community 

However, if that proves unsuccessful, you can create a case via Get Support (Cases, Expert Chat...) and submit it to SAP. 

Upon reaching SAP, first level support will investigate the issue. If a solution is already documented in an SAP Note or SAP Knowledge Based Article (KBA), the case will be returned to you with the attached solution, and your issue will hopefully be solved. 

If first level support cannot find a known solution, the case will be escalated to SAP developers at the second level of support. These developers investigate the issue, create, and test a solution, and document it in an SAP Note. This note is then provided to you through the case. Once the issue is resolved, you can close your case. 

SAP’s goal is to investigate and document a solution and share it with customers through SAP Notes and KBAs. This approach makes it much easier for you to find an immediate solution, reducing the time you are impacted by any issues with your SAP software. 

 

You have a case in SAP for Me and need to escalate this case? 

 

 

 

 

You can reach out to the Customer Interaction Center (CIC) to escalate your case. To request an escalation or a speed up in the processing of a support case you will be asked by the team to provide Business Impact. 

 

The importance of Business Impact 

 

Business impact serves as a tool used by SAP engineers to prioritize cases. If two cases are reported for the same technical issue, business impact helps determine which case should be processed first. It is a description of the issue from a non-technical perspective. 

When the Engineering Team is prioritising tickets or the Escalation are evaluating an escalation request, it is always the Business Impact Statement which is the focal point. 

 

Business Impact Questions 

 

Business impact questions are not technical and are related to the consequences the issue has on the company:  for example, for issues happening in Production these are some of the questions SAP would ask: 

 

Is core business functionality severely affected? 

Is there an acceptable workaround? 

How many users are affected? 

What is anticipated financial loss to the business due to the issue in question? 

 

Also please note that for Very High cases, you will be asked to provide a 24/7 contact person. SAP engineers will be working around the clock to solve the issue as quickly as possible and will need someone available to answer any details which may need to be confirmed with you. 

 

For a full list of business impact questions please refer to KBA 1281633 - Speed Up Processing of a Case - SAP for Me 

Also please refer to the KBA below to learn more about our escalation process  

90835 - SAP Case Escalation Procedure - SAP for Me 

 

Thanks for reading and we look forward to being able to assist you with any escalation or issue you may have!  

   

We hope you found this useful. Please note that the Customer Interaction Center is always happy to guide you on any of the mentioned processes and assist you with any queries you may have.   

If you have any questions, please post in the Q&A (Question & Answer) section here: All Questions in Customer Experience | SAP Community and All Questions in Support Services | SAP Community   

Read more #Customer Interaction Centre blogposts Here!

entry.title: IBP RTI - Cloud Connector high availability setup
entry.content:
Introduction

This blog is to write about the Idea to consume the high availability solution of SAP Cloud connector, where SAP ABAP application is calling the cloud services using RFC(Om-Premise to Cloud connectivity) using cloud connector.

With SAP cloud connector we have the high availability setup(Master - Shadow instances), which is more than enough for cloud to on-premise connections to have seamless connectivity, wherein calling the cloud services from on-premise we need additional setup to utilize the high availability setup, like some short of cluster or load balancers to identify and redirect the traffic always to master node.

Here we will discuss on using the Azure TCP load balancer between the SAP ABAP and SAP Cloud Connector and use TCP load balancer IP/FQDN in the SAP RFC connection.

Analysis based on various SAP Note, SAP community blogs and Microsoft learning blogs.

Pre-requisite

  1. SAP cloud connector 2.15.0 (Master Role Check monitoring API) required as Master role check monitoring API is available with version 2.15.0 onwards. 
  2. Azure TCP load balancer -> For traffic routing to master node based on the results of calling the Master Role Check monitoring API for the SAP cloud connector Nodes available in the backend pool using the health probes. 

Custom HTTP/HTTPS health probe for Azure Load Balancer will be required to translate the result(true) of Master Role check API to http status code 200 for marking the master node as healthy to route all the traffic to master node.

Solution

 

 

  1. Configure SAP Cloud Connector instance in high availability setup:
    • Set up two SAP Cloud Connector instances: a Master instance and a Shadow instance, both should be configured.
    • sapcc1 as master and sapcc2 as shadow instance 

    •  

       

    • Ensure both instances are registered and operational, with the Shadow instance ready to take over if the Master becomes unavailable. 
    • Configure a service channel to be called by ABAP client RFC.

       

      Target host used in the RFC will be the IP/FQDN of the TCP load balancer deployed in below steps. For our demo we used the instance as 85 on SAP RFC i.e. 3385 as service channel port

  2. Deploy a TCP Load Balancer in Azure: 
    • Create a Standard TCP Load Balancer in Azure, which can distribute traffic based on the TCP connections to either the Master or the Shadow SCC instance. 
    • Assign a Frontend IP: Choose whether to use a public or private IP, depending on whether the load balancer needs to be accessible internally or externally. 
    • Set up a Backend Pool: Add both the Master and Shadow SCC instances (or the respective VMs hosting these instances) to the backend pool.

       

  3. Set Up Health Probes for Failover:  
    • Create a HTTP health probe for monitoring the master SCC instances. Specify the custom URI/API to determine the master SCC instance (refer to custom health probe creation/configuration to consume the Master role check API available in SCC). Here we are using the custom API(Python API to consume SCC master role check API) hosted as service on the SCC VM's.

       

      Python sample code used and deployed as service and configured to start automatically-

from flask import Flask, jsonify
import requests
import logging

app = Flask(__name__)

# Configure logging
#logging.basicConfig(level=logging.DEBUG)

@app.route('/api/checkMasterRole', methods=['GET'])
def check_master_role():
    external_api_url = "https://<SAPCC hostname>:8443/exposed?action=hasMasterRole"
    
    try:
        response = requests.get(external_api_url, verify=False)  # Set verify=True for production
        response.raise_for_status()  # Raise an error for bad HTTP responses
        
        # Log the response content for debugging
        #app.logger.debug(f"Response content: {response.content}")
        
        # Check if the response is a string representation of a boolean
        response_text = response.text.strip().lower()
        
        if response_text == 'true':
            return jsonify({"status": "healthy"}), 200  # Return 200 if master role is true
        elif response_text == 'false':
            return jsonify({"status": "unhealthy", "reason": "Not master"}), 503  # Return 503 if not master
        else:
            #app.logger.error("Response is not a recognized boolean")
            return jsonify({"status": "error", "message": "Unexpected response format"}), 500

    except requests.exceptions.HTTPError as http_err:
        #app.logger.error(f"HTTP error occurred: {http_err}")
        return jsonify({"status": "unhealthy", "reason": "HTTP error"}), 503
    except Exception as e:
        #app.logger.error(f"An error occurred: {str(e)}")
        return jsonify({"status": "error", "message": "Internal Server Error"}), 500

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run(host='0.0.0.0', port=5000)
  • Configure the probe with suitable intervals (e.g., 5 seconds) and a threshold for the number of consecutive failures (e.g., 2) to ensure quick failover. 
  • Define Load Balancing Rules: 
    • Configure a Load Balancing Rule to map incoming TCP traffic from the frontend IP to the backend pool. Set the rule to use the same port that the ABAP client RFC uses to connect to SCC 33<nr> where nr is the instance no. mentioned on the ABAP client RFC. 

       

       

  • Test and Validate the Setup: 
    • Use Azure Monitor to track metrics and validate the health probe statuses.

       

       

    • Change the ABAP client RFC connection to use the fqdn/IP of the load balancer in hostname.
    • Test the setup by accessing the service channel using the RFC connection from the ABAP client system. The TCP Load Balancer should direct traffic to the Master instance by default. 
    • Simulate a failure on the Master SCC instance to verify that the load balancer correctly routes traffic to the Shadow SCC instance.
    • sapcc1 as shadow and sapcc2 as master.
    •  

       

       

       

       

       

Reference articles- 

https://help.sap.com/docs/connectivity/sap-btp-connectivity-cf/configure-service-channel-for-rfc 

https://d.dam.sap.com/a/QJYXmLy/RTI_Communcation_Setup_Guide_v.2.5.pdf?rc=67&inline=true

3439494 - IBP RTI - Cloud Connector high availability setup 

https://help.sap.com/docs/connectivity/sap-btp-connectivity-cf/monitoring-apis 

https://help.sap.com/docs/connectivity/sap-btp-connectivity-cf/monitoring-apis#loiof6e7a7bc6af345d2a... 

https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/load-balancer/ 

https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/load-balancer/load-balancer-custom-probe-overview 

https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/load-balancer/create-custom-http-health-probe-howto

entry.title: My Journey Through Devtoberfest 2024: Learning, Connecting, and Growing with SAP
entry.content:
English Version.

After returning from my September vacation, I started seeing LinkedIn posts about Devtoberfest 2024. Although I’d heard about it in previous years, I had never taken the time to look into whether it could actually help me in the future.

After reading one of those posts, I decided to register and experience it firsthand to see what it was all about, what activities it offered, and how it could benefit my future in SAP.

So, I signed up and prepared myself for the four weeks of Devtoberfest, with the goal of exploring how far I could go. During the event, there was a break for the Teched online, another key week for learning about SAP's future.

Each day of the week was dedicated to a specific topic:

  • Monday: ABAP & CAP
  • Tuesday: Tooling
  • Wednesday: Integration
  • Thursday: MAD (Machine Learning, AI, and Data)
  • Friday: Frontend

On September 23, the activities for the first week began. Although I wasn’t entirely sure what the event or the activity dynamics would be like, I was determined to seize the opportunity and learn about the latest SAP innovations.

Before each week ended, the activities for the following week would be unlocked, with links provided for easy access to each one. There were also video sessions featuring SAP experts sharing their knowledge, followed by questions to reinforce learning. However, what I enjoyed most were the Friday activities, which were designed to foster community interaction: sharing photos of our pets, our workspaces, or finding bugs in different languages, and even uploading Halloween-themed photos.

In conclusion, I think anyone in the field of SAP development should participate in Devtoberfest at least once. It’s a very well-organized event with a lot to learn. Personally, I discovered tools like SAP BUILD, which allows the creation of applications, workflows, and forms without needing coding skills — the trendy Low-code No-code approach. I also learned about Clean Core, ABAP for Cloud, Integration Suite, and Fiori for the frontend. These four weeks not only helped me improve my skills but also allowed me to connect with the community and meet SAP specialists.

A big thank you to @thomas_jung  for organizing the event, to @ajmaradiaga for his posts that motivated me to participate in Devtoberfest 2024, and to all the experts who shared their knowledge.

I finished the event with a score of 45,710 points, and now I eagerly await to see if I'll be one of the lucky ones to receive a special prize!

Spanish Version.

Después de las vacaciones de septiembre, empecé a ver publicaciones en LinkedIn sobre el Devtoberfest 2024. Aunque había oído hablar de él en años anteriores, nunca me había detenido a investigar si realmente podría ayudarme en el futuro.

Al leer una de esas publicaciones, decidí inscribirme y experimentarlo desde dentro para ver en qué consistía, qué actividades ofrecía y cómo podría beneficiarme en mi futuro en SAP.

Así que me apunté y me preparé para las cuatro semanas del Devtoberfest, con la intención de explorar hasta dónde podría llegar. Durante el evento, hubo una pausa para el Teched online, otra semana clave para conocer el futuro de SAP.

Cada día de la semana estaba dedicado a un tema específico:

  • Lunes: ABAP & CAP
  • Martes: Tooling
  • Miércoles: Integration
  • Jueves: MAD (Machine Learning, AI and Data)
  • Viernes: Frontend

El 23 de septiembre comenzaron las actividades de la primera semana. Aunque no tenía claro cómo sería el evento ni las dinámicas de las actividades, estaba decidido a aprovechar la oportunidad y aprender las novedades que SAP ofrece.

Antes de finalizar cada semana, se activaban las actividades de la siguiente, proporcionando enlaces para acceder a cada una sin problemas. También había sesiones en video con expertos de SAP que compartían su conocimiento, seguidas de preguntas para reforzar el aprendizaje. Sin embargo, lo que más disfruté fueron las actividades de los viernes, diseñadas para fomentar la interacción en la comunidad: compartir fotos con nuestras mascotas, de nuestros espacios de trabajo o buscar errores en diferentes lenguajes, y hasta subir fotos temáticas de Halloween.

En conclusión, creo que cualquier persona en el ámbito de desarrollo en SAP debería participar al menos una vez en el Devtoberfest. Es un evento muy bien organizado donde se aprende muchísimo. Yo, personalmente, descubrí herramientas como SAP BUILD, que permite crear aplicaciones, flujos de trabajo y formularios sin necesidad de programación, el Low-code No code de moda. Además, aprendí sobre el Clean Core, ABAP para Cloud, Integration Suite, y Fiori para el frontend. Estas cuatro semanas me permitieron no solo mejorar mis habilidades, sino también conectar con la comunidad y conocer a especialistas de SAP.

Gracias a @thomas_jung  por la organización del evento, a @ajmaradiaga  por sus publicaciones que me motivaron a participar en Devtoberfest 2024, y a todos los expertos que compartieron su conocimiento.

Finalicé el evento con una puntuación de 45,710 puntos, ¡y ahora espero con emoción si seré uno de los afortunados en recibir un premio especial!

entry.title: Author Spotlight: Tim Koster - November 2024 Edition
entry.content:
We notice the countless posts published daily in SAP Community, and we notice you!  

We appreciate your continuous engagement and activity in SAP Community and want to highlight your contributions.  

Starting today, we are launching a new initiative to recognize authors who consistently publish blog posts, answer questions, attend events, and demonstrate their passion as active members of SAP Community.  

Read our first interview with Tim Koster, a member since 2016. Discover his background, experiences, and interests. This is all about Tim! 

Svea: Hi Tim, How and when did you get in SAP Community?

Tim: I actually had to look that up. I started in 2016 at Interdobs, the company that I still work for, and there I met @RonaldKonijnenb. He is an SAP Mentor and a friend of mine. He basically introduced me to the SAP Community.

I can remember that he got an Intel NUC from the SAP Mentor program. It was just before SAP TechEd and we had some great fun with that, because we tried out various things. Ronald was excited of one of my projects and mentioned that I should publish the outcome in the SAP Community. And that's where it all started, basically.

Svea: That sounds really like fun. What does the community mean to you?

Tim: For me it’s collaboration, the openness, and the welcoming fact. There's no real obligation so you can share if you want, but if you don't want to and you just want to listen, that's fine as well. If you don't want to publish blog posts, but you just want to read or you just want to comment or ask a question, everyone is free to do so, basically. There is no obligation for example that you first have to publish 10 blogs before you can actually ask a question, or so. Really everyone is welcome. And yeah, that's great.

Svea: I agree. What inspires you most?

Tim: In November of the same year, when I started in SAP Community, I participated in my first SAP Inside Track in the Netherlands, so called sitNL. I was surprised how that whole event turned out, who showed up, the presentations, the openness, and casual talks. I was not used to it as in my previous company, it has been seen differently. They saw the whole world as a competitor basically. But at an SAP Inside Track all these competitors are sharing knowledge with each other, everyone is talking to each other and is friendly with each other. That was a really positive surprise for me.

It’s actually also a mix of SAP employees and consultants, clients, even competitors who are participating in these events. Basically, everyone is there, and everyone can get involved if they want. It really is a community.

Svea: That is a great experience that you made at sitNL. Do you want to talk about your main expertise and background?

Tim: I have a completely different background. After I've got my first diploma, I started to study to be a maritime officer and after the third year I found out that I'm chronically seasick. During my internship I was on board of one of those huge container ships, and yeah, what shall I say: I was basically ill all the time. So that was my dream I lived, but it turned into a nightmare.

Then I made the change to HR, human resource management, and I finished my bachelor there. I started as a data analyst at an HR department. Quickly, I found out that I was good at Excel, and if you're good with Excel within HR, you quickly own business intelligence within that department. I also learnt business intelligence with Microsoft tools. Thankfully, someone I met invited me to Interdobs, and I have worked there for eight years now.

Svea: I am sorry to hear you could not continue living your dream. But it also sounds like you enjoy what you are doing now.

Is there any “highlight” you want to share?

Tim: For me the highlight of every year is sitNL. It’s frankly speaking always difficult to arrange this within my family, as here in the Netherlands, also Sinterklaas (Santa Clause) is arriving at the same day. But they know how important it is for me, so luckily, I am always able to go there. And when I am there it's nice to see everyone again, but it's also nice to present there and listen to other people's stories. I also see the amount of time that is invested by all organizers. I know from Ronald how much time goes into organizing such an event, finding speakers for instance, and I also know that there's a lot of costs involved. And I am very thankful that the organizers put in all that time and effort.

I'm always quite surprised by the people that show up not only as participants, but also as speakers because, as I mentioned earlier, even competitors of SAP are now showing up to present to the audience on a Saturday morning about their connection to the SAP world. And that's also quite cool, yeah.

Svea: Do you have any plans for your future in SAP Community?​

Tim: Basically, I've been nominated for the SAP Champions and also for the SAP Mentors Program. I'm honored to join either of these and give my feedback and ideas to Product Management and Product Development, as I have some great ideas *wink wink*. But if I don’t end up in that role I for sure will keep blogging. I will keep responding. Of course it's nice to have that recognition, but it's probably not why I am doing it.

Svea: What would you like to get from SAP Community or other community members?

Tim: I hope that SAP keeps investing in SAP Community because I think it's really important and it makes a difference compared to the other bigger parties like Microsoft, Google and Amazon, that have communities, but it does not feel the same. SAP Community sometimes feels like family, especially at those event days. I mean, it’s different to have dinner with people that you only interact with virtually but see once a year. Basically, some of them even come from the UK or other parts of the world. You feel connected with them.

Sharing knowledge in the SAP Community is just great and it gives a certain fulfillment and I hope that it's the same for everyone. I hope that everyone feels that they are just allowed to share whatever they have to share. It can be just a small thing, or it can be huge.

Svea: Is there anything else you would like to share?

Tim: I'm happy with SAP Community, also with the new platform. Especially, I do like the fact that you can now see the blog posts about datasphere in the SAP Datasphere area, because this helps to find stuff.

I'm thankful for the community team, that you keep the SAP Community alive and running, and I hope you will continue.

I am also thankful that you invited me. I hope that this interview gives a boost to other people to join in and to share knowledge and engage in SAP Community. Because this is not about me, it's about the community!

Svea: The pleasure was on my side, Tim. I am glad you are part of SAP Community and I am looking forward to seeing your story continuing.

 

I hope you enjoyed Tim's story. Stay tuned for the next blog post in December—perhaps you'll be one of the active authors we highlight next!

entry.title: SAP Named a Top 50 DEI Team at 2024 OnCon Icon Awards
entry.content:
We are thrilled to announce that SAP's Global Diversity & Inclusion Team has been honored with a prestigious Top 50 DEI Team award at the 2024 OnCon Icon Awards! Usually, we recognize awards at the country or regional level within our organization, but this accolade truly highlights the strength of our inclusive culture on a global scale. This award is a testament to our continuous efforts and success in promoting diversity and inclusion across all levels of our organization.

As of October 2024, a remarkable 40 out of 105 Employer of Choice awards have been dedicated to Diversity & Inclusion, underscoring our unwavering commitment to creating workplaces that celebrate every voice and uplift every individual’s contribution. 

The OnCon Icon Awards are quite unique as the winners are selected by the public. Voters chose teams they felt combined a successful track record with a positive organizational impact. Our D&I team stood out for its significant contributions through thought-leadership, innovation in projects, and exceptional leadership.

This prestigious award highlights the exceptional efforts and unwavering dedication of our team in fostering a truly inclusive and equitable culture at SAP. Our commitment inspires us to drive meaningful initiatives every day, ensuring that everyone within our organization feels valued and empowered. This recognition not only affirms our hard work but also enhances our reputation as a D&I leader, making SAP an attractive choice for top-tier talent who prioritize these values.

 

The GDIO team extend its thanks to all the Executive Board Members, Employee Network Groups, Regional D&I Leads and every ally at SAP for their relentless hard work and passion in driving the D&I agenda. We are deeply grateful for the recognition of our efforts, and your dedication is truly the heartbeat of our success. We eagerly anticipate many more milestones and achievements together!

entry.title: Coach's Corner - Be Water and Let Your Career Flow
entry.content:
The phrase "Be water" is most famously associated with Bruce Lee, the martial artist, movie star and philosopher. He shared this idea during an interview in 1971, where he explained the concept of adaptability and flexibility. His full quote is:

"Empty your mind, be formless, shapeless—like water. Now you put water in a cup, it becomes the cup; You put water into a bottle, it becomes the bottle; You put it in a teapot, it becomes the teapot. Now water can flow or it can crash. Be water, my friend."

(image generated by AI)

 
Bruce Lee was influenced by Taoism. Taoism, particularly through its foundational text, the *Tao Te Ching* by Laozi, heavily emphasizes the concept of "being like water" and "going with the flow." This philosophy emphasizes being adaptable, flexible, and able to adjust to different circumstances, much like water takes the shape of whatever contains it.
 
The parallel I'd like to draw between our careers and being like water comes from a recent conversation I had with a senior recruiter at SAP. One of the most challenging part of his job was to explain to job seekers (especially young applicants within the same organization) why they weren't picked to interview for a position that was 2 or 3 pay-grades higher than their current role. Getting an interview in this circumstance is rare, and the chances of getting the job is even slimmer. His advice for job seekers is to have the right expectations and get to know the process. This advice is basic but essential, and isn't unique to SAP but applies to almost all seeking to climb the corporate ladder in any organization. 
 
Every organization has a flow - a rhythm, cycle or way of doing things. It has established processes, schedules and systems to regulate hiring, assess skills, provide training, manage performance, distribute pay, promotions and raises, and plan for leadership succession. By knowing what the rhythm is and adapting your expectations and actions to fit this flow, you will not only be more successful but also encounter greater satisfaction with your career trajectory. 
 
If you've ever done the opposite and tried to go against the established process within a company, you'll know it is like swimming against a river's current. The likely outcome is greater fatigue and missing your target destination. Job seekers and employees who expect and push for changes off-cycle to the company's flow often end up exhausted and frustrated with the results. Sure, it's possible for an intern to become CEO in the same organization, but the chances are miniscule and I usually advise a more pragmatic and achievable goal for my clients.

Be Content Without Being Complacent

 
While "going with the flow" inherently requires trust and patience with the system we're in, there is also a large dose of appreciation and contentment we must learn to adopt within ourselves. The pull-through benefit of adopting this approach is the reduction of stress and pressure we feel from unrealistic expectations (whether created by ourselves or by others) and better management of our scarcity mindset.
 
Being content with our current condition does not mean being stagnant in pursuing our personal aspirations and career growth. To move from a 'scarcity mindset' to an 'abundance mindset,' we must proactively manage our thoughts while intentionally managing our behaviours.
 
Here are 3 ways to remain content without being complacent:
  1. Continuous learning - One of the first things to learn is the timing and schedule of everything in your organization or the organization you're interested in working for. The goal is to understand how processes such as hiring, promotions, transfers and succession planning work in the organization. I would be more interested in adapting my approach to the organizational flow, than to find back-doors, exceptions and ways to game the system.
  2. Build your network and relationships - The best way I can recommend learning about a company's flow is from your network. This means building new connections, reconnect with those you've loss touch with, maintaining your relationships. Even though career networking sites (like Glassdoor and LinkedIn) are available, speaking directly to your network of friends and acquaintances is the fastest and most trusted way to learn about a company's flow. Stay curious and ask lots of questions, and you might find it enjoyable.
  3. Find help - Whether it's a coach or a mentor, you can fast-track your efforts when you have a trusted advisor close by to support and sanity-check your plan. A good coach or mentor will not let you forget your strengths and accomplishments, and will encourage you to apply those experiences to achieve your short term and long term goals. They will also help you take stock of your achievements and put expectations into perspective. Ask for help to develop a strategy and tactics to use the company's flow to get you across faster, safely and on-track to reach your career destination.
Embracing the philosophy of "being like water" in your career means adapting to your organization's flow, understanding its processes, and aligning your expectations with its rhythm. By doing so, you'll not only increase your chances of success but also find greater satisfaction in your career journey. Remember, being content doesn't mean being complacent. Continuous learning, building your network, and seeking help from mentors or coaches are proactive ways to maintain an abundance mindset and propel your career forward. So, be water, my friend, and let your career flow.

(Blog reposted from https://stonesoupcoaching.wordpress.com)
